titleStmt.title: Gulo Maḵadā, ʿĀddaqāḥarsi Ṗarāqliṭos, AP-016
Gadla ḥawāryāt, Apocryphal Acts of the Apostles / Taʾammǝra Mikāʾel “Miracles of Michael” / Homily on St Michael (story about St Talāson)

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msContents.summary:
I) Gadla ḥawāryāt, Apocryphal Acts of the Apostles (fols. 1ra-11rb, 12ra-166vb) I-1) The Discourse of Dionysius on the Martyrdom of Sts. Peter and Paul, with colophon (fols. 1ra-11vb) I-2) Introduction (fols. 12ra-12va) I-3) The Preaching of St Peter (fols. 12va-23rb) I-4) The Martyrdom of St Peter (fols. 23va-27vb) I-5) The Martyrdom of St Paul (fols. 28ra-30ra) I-6) The Preaching of St Simon the son of Cleopas, surnamed Judas or Nathaniel (fols. 30ra-31vb) I-7) The Martyrdom of St Simon the son of Cleopas, surnamed Judas or Nathaniel (fols. 31vb-32vb) I-8) The Preaching of St James the Just (fols. 33ra-34rb) I-9) The Martyrdom of St James the Just (fols. 34rb-36vb) I-10) The Preaching of St Bartholomew (fols. 36vb-41ra) I-11) The Martyrdom of St Bartholomew (fols. 41rb-43va) I-12) The Acts of St Matthew (fols. 43va-50rb) I-13) The Martyrdom of St Matthew (fols. 50ra-52rb) I-14) The Martyrdom of St Luke (fols. 52rb-55ra) I-15) The Preaching of St Philip (fols. 55ra-59ra) I-16) The Martyrdom of St Philip (fols. 59ra-61rb) I-17) The Preaching of St Andrew (fols. 61va-69va) I-18) The Acts of Sts. Andrew and Bartholomew (fols. 69va-82rb) I-19) The Acts of St John the Son of Zebedee (fols. 83ra-95va) I-20) The Death of St John the Son of Zebedee (fols. 95va-99rb) I-21) The Martyrdom of St James the Son of Alphaeus (fols. 99rb-100ra) I-22) The Preaching of St Matthias (fols. 100ra-109vb)
I-23) The Martyrdom of St Matthias (fols. 109vb-112vb) I-24) The Acts of St James the Son of Zebedee (fols. 112vb-115vb) I-25) The Martyrdom of St James the Son of Zebedee, with colophon (fols. 115vb-117vb) I-26) The Martyrdom of St Mark the Evangelist (fols. 118ra-120va) I-27) The Preaching of St Thomas (fols. 120va-128vb) I-28) The Martyrdom of St Thomas (fols. 129ra-132ra) I-29) The Preaching of St Judas Thaddeus (fols. 132ra-135vb) I-30) The Acts of Sts. Matthias and Andrew, presented as Acts of St Andrew (fols. 136ra-147va) I-31) The Acts of St Thomas, I (fols 148ra-152vb) I-32) The Acts of St Thomas, II (fols. 152vb-157rb) I-33) The Acts of St Thomas, III (fols. 157rb-161va)
I-34) The Acts of St Thomas, IV (fols. 161va-163vb) I-35) The Acts of St Thomas, V (fols. 163vb-166vb) II) Taʾammǝra Mikāʾel “Miracles of Michael”: 12 miracles (fols. 167ra-174vb) III) Homily on Archangel Michael (story about St Talāson) (fols. 175ra-180rb).

msItem.title: Contendings of the Apostles: Recension IV

bibl.title: The apocryphal Acts of the Apostles: unknown witnesses from East Tegray

Essays in Ethiopian Manuscript Studies. Proceedings of the International Conference Manuscripts and Texts, Languages and Contexts: the Transmission of Knowledge in the Horn of Africa, Hamburg, 17-19 July 2014
Supplement to Aethiopica

msItem.note: - According to the distribution of the texts, it would belong to 4 recension (cp. Gädlä ḥawaryatEAeEncyclopaedia AethiopicaAlessandroBausiSiegbertUhligWiesbadenHarrassowitz Verlag2005II1049b-1051abm:Bausi2005Hawaryatbm:Bausi2005Hawaryat, 83-84).
- Most of the texts are separated by a chain of red and black dots with dashes in between.

msItem.title: Discourse of Dionysius on the Martyrdom of Sts Peter and Paul
msItem.note: - Text I-1 is separated from the following ones by a blank folio. It ends with a subscriptio (supplication) separated from the main text by a chain of black and red dots with dashes in between.

msItem.title: Introduction
The Preaching of St Peter
The Martyrdom of St Peter
Discourse of Peter on the Cross
The Martyrdom of St Paul
The Preaching of St Simon the son of Cleopas, surnamed Judas or Nathaniel
The Martyrdom of St Simon the son of Cleopas, surnamed Judas or Nathaniel
The Preaching of St James the Just
The Martyrdom of St James the Just
The Preaching of St Bartholomew
The Martyrdom of St Bartholomew
The Acts of St Matthew
The Martyrdom of St Matthew
The Martyrdom of St Luke
The Preaching of St Philip
The Martyrdom of St Philip
The Preaching of St Andrew
The Acts of Sts Andrew and Bartholomew
The Acts of St John the Son of Zebedee
The Death of St John the Son of Zebedee
The Martyrdom of St James the Son of Alphaeus
The Preaching of St Matthias
The Martyrdom of St Matthias
The Acts of St James the Son of Zebedee
The Martyrdom of St James the Son of Zebedee
msItem.note: - Text I-25 ends with a subscriptio (note on the the commissioners and supplication).

msItem.title: The Martyrdom of St Mark the Evangelist
The Preaching of St Thomas
The Martyrdom of St Thomas
The Preaching of St Judas Thaddeus
The Acts of Sts Matthias and Andrew, presented as Acts of St Andrew
The Acts of St Thomas, I
The Acts of St Thomas, II
The Acts of St Thomas, III
The Acts of St Thomas, IV
The Acts of St Thomas, V
Taʾammǝra Mikāʾel “Miracles of Michael”: 12 miracles
msItem.note: the miracles are separated from the each other by a chain of red and black dots with dashes in between. The text ends with tree subscriptiones separated by chain of red and black dots with dashes in between.
msItem.explicit: three extensive subscriptiones : supplication for commissioners; supplication for commissioners, scribe, reader etc.; condemnation against those who take the book away

msItem.title: Homily on Archangel Michael (story about Talāson)
msItem.incipit: ድርሳን፡ ዘቅዱስ፡ ወብፁዕ፡ አባ፡ ደማቲዮስ፡ ጳጳስ፡ ዘኢየሩሳሌም፡ ዘደረሰ፡ በእንተ፡ ሚካኤል፡ ሊቀ፡ መላእክት፡ ገባሬ፡ ተአምር፡ ወመንክር፡ ወበእንተ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወንጹሕ፡ መፍቀሬ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ተላሶን፡ ዘአምነ፡ በእግዚእነ፡ ወመድኀኒነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ወልደ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ሕያው፡ በሰላመ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ አብ፡ አሜን። ... According to the incipit the homily was composed by (Timothy) Damātiyos, Metropolitan of Jerusalem.

supportDesc.extent: Made of 182.0 folios, of which blank 11v, 82v, 180v, 181rv, 182rv, in 26.0 quires (25+B). 360 240 100

list.item: 8 VI(8; s.l.: 3, stub after 5; 6, stub after 2 /fols. 41r - 48v)
8 VII(8; s.l.: 3, stub after 6; 6, stub after 3/fols. 49r - 56v)

11
XVIII(11; s.l.: 6, stub after 3; 8, stub after 2; 11, stub before 1/fols. 126r-135v)

supportDesc.condition: The front cover is broken and repaired. The Ms. has been resewn; the sewing is damaged. Some leaves are affected by humidity in the inner upper margins (esp. 2r-19r, 58rv). Holes on 139, 140, 141, 147, 178, 179, 180, 181, 182. Cuts carefully amended, 19, 56, 61, 62. Some leaves are cut: 82 (the bottom margin), 134, 172-173, 180 (the outer margin). 176-182 as well as the back board are damaged at the upper edge (probably by rodents).

layout.ab: The number of lines is irregular, varying greatly, between 22 and 31
The upper line is written below the ruling.
The bottom line is written above the ruling (in a few cases also below, e.g., 55v, 56r).

handNote.seg: A few lines alternating with black lines in the incipit page of Texts I, II and III; the incipit of each text within Text I and sometimes one or two lines of the account proper; one or two lines in the incipit of each miracle in Text-II; trinitarian formula; Ethiopic numerals or their elements; elements of the punctuation signs and of coronis signs.

The handwriting is characterized by upright to slightly left-sloping letters, uneven and not uniform (also number of the lines pro page is not constant), at times creating an impression of more than hands involved in writing. In 61r, 71vb, 72va, the handwriting appears different: broader strokes, less carefully executed with a different tone of black ink.
- መ (esp. in the 1st and 2nd orders) has loops of triangular shape, with the left loop more sharply pointed
- the upper horizontal strokes of some letters (e.g., መ ሠ በ) are uniformly slanted to the left
- some letters have rounded upper strokes, e.g., the letter ገ in the 6th order
- the 6th order marker of ጰ is set up on the vertical stroke at the top and oriented to the left (e.g., 11ra l.24, 45va l.19)

- the 6th order marker of the letter የ has a very short and downwards oriented left part, and a long, upwards oriented right part (e.g., 45vb l.5); in the 4th order, the marker is diagonal and going down below the ruling (e.g., 45vb l.7); the letters ኀ and ቀ in the 4th order show the same feature
- the loop marking the 7th order of letter ቀ is very pronounced, broad and elevates above other signs
- ፲ appears occasionally in the form with a ring attached to the right side through a horizontal stroke (135vb l.7).

item.desc: - The commemoration dates of the Apostles or notes summarizing the content are written in red and black (by the same hand as that of the main text) in the upper margin (a few times also in the lateral margins) of some fols.: ግንዘቱ፡ ወስምዑ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡: fol. 23v አመ፡ ፲ለሐምሌ፡: fol. 30r አም፬ለታኅሣሥ፡ እንድርያስ፡: fol. 61v በዘ፡ ገብረ፡ እንድርያስ፡: fol. 69v አመ፡ ፬፡ ለወርኀ፡ ጥር፡ [n.l.] ዮሐንስ፡ ወልደ፡ ዘብዴዎስ፡: fol. 83r አመ፡ ፲ለየካቲት፡ ያዕቆብ፡ ወልደ፡ እልፍዮስ፡: fol. 99r አመ፡ ፰ለመጋቢት፡ ማቲያስ፡: fol. 100r አመ፡ ሰሙኑ፡ ለመጋቢት፡: fol. 109v አመ፡ ፲፡ ወ፡ [n.l.] ለሚያዝያ፡: fol. 112v አመ፡ ፴ለሚያዝያ፡: fol. 118r አመ፡ ፳፡ ወ፯፡ ለግንቦት።: fol. 120v አመ፡ ፪፡ ለሔ[sic]ምሌ፡: fol. 132r አመ፡ ፴፡ ለነኃሴ፡: fol. 136r አመ፡ ፲፡ ወ፰፡ ለመስከረም።: fol. 148r አመ፡ ፱፡ ለጥቅምት፡: fol. 152v (written twice) አመ፡ ፪ለያከቲት[sic]፡: fol. 157r ግብር፡ ፫: fol. 157r (right margin) አመ፡ ፲፬ለመጋቢት፡: fol. 161v ግብር፡ ፬: fol. 161v (left margin) አመ፡ [n.l.] (above); ግብር፡ ፭ (below): fol. 163v

- monthly readings for Text II are indicated in red in the upper margin: እም፡ ፲ወ፪ለስኔ፡ ምንበብ፡: fol. 167r አመ፡ ፲ወ፪ለኀምሌ፡ ምንበብ፡; አመ፡ ፲ወ፪ለነሐሲ፡ ምንበብ፡: fol. 167v በመስከረም፡ ምንበብ፡: fol. 168r አመ፡ ፲ወ፪፡ ለጥቅምት፡: fol. 168v አመ፡ ፲ወ፪ለኀዳር።: fol. 169r አመ፡ ፲ወ፪፡ ለታኅሣሥ፡: fol. 170r አመ፡ ፲ወ፪፡ ለጥር፡: fol. 170v አመ፡ ፲ወ፪ለየኪ[sic]ቲት፡: fol. 171r አመ፡ ፲ወ፪ለመጋቢት፡: fol. 171v አመ፡ ፲ወ፪ለሚያዝያ፡: fol. 172r አመ፡ ፲ወ፪፡ ለግንቦት።: fol. 173r
- the date of monthly reading for the “Homily of Timothy” is indicated in red in the upper margin of fol. 175r: አመ፡ ፲፪፡ ለታኅሣሥ፡
Correction written over erasure: e.g., fols. 44rb, 46rb, 47rb, 49ra, 70va.
In some fols., the scribe did not use the upper or the bottom ruled lines, e.g., fols. 1r, 45r, 83rv, 85r, 115r, 176r. In fol. 117rb one ruled line is empty between the explicit and the subscriptio.
On fol. 135v (after Text I-29), a chain of coronis with a chain of red and black dots and dashes below and above.
The paragraph sign is used especially on fols. 2v and 91r.
- Compressed script (in red ink of a different tone?) is used in rubrications: e.g., fols. 43va, 50rb.
A drawing of the cross (or doodle?) in the bottom margin of fol. 44r.

binding.decoNote: Two wooden boards (the front board is broken and repaired). Two pairs of sewing stations. A piece of textile (cotton, gauze?) is crudely sewn between the front board and the first leaf (likely as a fly-leaf).

history.provenance: Gabaz-priests (qasawǝsta gabaz) Bakʷǝra Ṣǝyon, Manqorewos (sic), Tasfā Ḥǝywat are mentioned as commissioners (zǝntu gadla ḥawāryāt za-Ṗārāqliṭos za-ʾaṣḥafu...) in the subscriptio to Text I-1 (117vb ls. 3-5 ). A subscriptio to Text II (174va) states that it was commissioned (ʾagbaru) by the priests of the Ṗārāqliṭos.

bibl.title: The apocryphal Acts of the Apostles: unknown witnesses from East Tegray

Essays in Ethiopian Manuscript Studies. Proceedings of the International Conference Manuscripts and Texts, Languages and Contexts: the Transmission of Knowledge in the Horn of Africa, Hamburg, 17-19 July 2014
Supplement to Aethiopica

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: transformed from mycore to TEI P5
